chunks.page_summary: This page explains the meaning and structure of IP (Ingress Protection) ratings, detailing what each digit signifies for protection against solids and water, and how it applies to Rheonics products.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

IP rating explained
Modified on: Wed, 29 May, 2024 at 8:26 PM

What products are involved?
All Rheonics sensors, transmitters and cables that have an IP rating.

What is the purpose of this article?
To explain the meaning of IP ratings, its digits and the protection levels Rheonics products have.

1. IP Ratings overview

IP (Ingress Protection or International Protection Mark) from IEC60529 (https://webstore.iec.ch/publication/2452), rates the protection against instrusion of solids, accidental contact, dust, water and steam into mechanical housings and electrical enclosures.
Categories are based on the use of two digits, which have specific meanings each, as follows.

First Digit: Protection against solids.
0: No protection
1: Protection to active or internal parts against solids greater than Ø50mm (e.g. hand contact), not against intentional access.
2: Protection against solids greater than Ø12mm (e.g. finger contact).
3: Protection against solids greater than Ø2.5mm (e.g. screwdriver, tools).
4: Protection against solids greater than 1mm (e.g. thin wires).
5: Protection against harmful amount of dust for 2-8 hours.
6: Total protection against dust and any solids.

Second Digit: Protection against water.
0: No protection
1: Protection against vertically falling drops of water. Tested for 10 minutes.
2: Protection against vertically and up to 15° tilted falling drops of water. Tested for 10 minutes.
3: Protection against vertically and up to 60° tilted falling drops of water (e.g. spray water) Tested for 5 minutes.
4: Protection against water splashed from any direction.
5: Protection against jets of water. Tested for at least 15 minutes.
6: Protection against harmful quantity of water from heavy seas or jets of water.
6K: Protection against harmful powerful jets of water from any direction and elevated pressure.
7: Protection against immersion in water from 15cm to 100cm for 30 minutes.
8: Protection against immersion in water for long periods under harsh conditions (e.g. pressure).
9K: Protection against high pressure water or steam.

Example: IP67

Figure 1: IP rating digits meaning.

2. What does Rheonics offer?

Rheonics sensors are divided in three main parts, the sensor electronics, cable and probe. Each have an specific IP rating as shown in next image.

specifications.IP First Digit 3: Protection against solids greater than Ø2.5mm
specifications.IP First Digit 5: Protection against harmful amount of dust for 2-8 hours
specifications.IP First Digit 6: Total protection against dust and any solids

specifications.IP Second Digit 3: Protection against vertically and up to 60° tilted falling drops of water (e.g. spray water) Tested for 5 minutes.
specifications.IP Second Digit 4: Protection against water splashed from any direction.
specifications.IP Second Digit 5: Protection against jets of water. Tested for at least 15 minutes.
specifications.IP Second Digit 6: Protection against harmful quantity of water from heavy seas or jets of water.
specifications.IP Second Digit 6K: Protection against harmful powerful jets of water from any direction and elevated pressure.
specifications.IP Second Digit 7: Protection against immersion in water from 15cm to 100cm for 30 minutes.
specifications.IP Second Digit 8: Protection against immersion in water for long periods under harsh conditions (e.g. pressure).
specifications.IP Second Digit 9K: Protection against high pressure water or steam.

key_facts: The IP (Ingress Protection or International Protection Mark) system, derived from IEC60529, rates the protection against intrusion of solids, accidental contact, dust, water, and steam into mechanical housings and electrical enclosures.
IP rating categories are based on two digits, each having specific meanings for protection levels.
This article aims to explain IP ratings, their digits, and the protection levels Rheonics products have.
All Rheonics sensors, transmitters, and cables that have an IP rating are covered by this explanation.
Rheonics sensors are divided into three main parts: the sensor electronics, cable, and probe, each having a specific IP rating.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the IP protection ratings for various Rheonics sensor electronics, cables, connectors, and probes, showcasing specific models like SRV inline viscometers and SRD inline density and viscosity probes.

key_facts: Rheonics sensor electronics models SME-TR and SME-TRD have an IP 66 rating.
The SME-DRM sensor electronics model has an IP 20 rating.
The sensor cable provided is a standard cable with a PUR sheath.
The M12 8-pin A-coded connector is available with IP68 and IP69 ratings, with IP69K connectors also available and frequently used in the food industry.
Connectors are offered in both straight 180° and angled 90° versions.
The SRV probe is hermetically sealed and has an IP69K rating.
Rheonics SR-sensor probes, including SRV and SRD, have an IP69K rating.
Table 1 (referenced but not included on this page) provides more detailed protection ratings and information on Rheonics products.
The SRV IP69K is an inline viscometer, and other process connection variants are available.
The SRD IP69K is an inline density and viscosity probe, and other process connection variants are available.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the IP protection ratings and descriptions for various Rheonics sensor devices, along with considerations for their installation and cabling requirements to ensure proper environmental protection.
chunks.extracted_text:
Review all Rheonics sensor process connection variants here (https://rheonics.com/process-connections-rheonics-viscometer-and-density-meter/).

Table 1: Rheonics devices and IP ratings

Rheonics Devices: SME-DRM (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/), sensor electronics for mounting on DIN rail, used in cabinets
IP Rating: IP40
Protection level description: Protection against solids greater than 1 mm. No protection against water and any fluid.
Notes/Considerations: For indoors installations or to be installed in cabinets.

Rheonics Devices: SME-TR (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/), sensor electronics in transmitter housing
IP Rating: IP66
Protection level description: Total protection against intrusion of dust and solids. Protection against temporary flooding.
Notes/Considerations: For indoors and outdoors installations.

Rheonics Devices: SME-TRD (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/), sensor electronics in transmitter enclosure with display
IP Rating: IP66
Protection level description: Total protection against intrusion of dust and solids. Protection against temporary flooding.
Notes/Considerations: For indoors and outdoors installations.

Rheonics Devices: SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), inline viscometer (all variants)
IP Rating: IP69K
Protection level description: Sensor probe body is hermetically sealed and offers complete protection against intrusion of dust, solids and high pressure water or steam into the internal components.
Notes/Considerations: Sensor cable and M12 connector with IP69K rating are needed for complete protection.

Rheonics Devices: SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), inline density and viscosity probe
IP Rating: IP69K
Protection level description: Sensor probe body is hermetically sealed and offers complete protection against intrusion of dust, solids and high pressure water or steam into the internal components.
Notes/Considerations: Sensor cable and M12 connector with IP69K rating are needed for complete protection.

Rheonics Devices: DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/), HPHT density and viscosity meter
IP Rating: IP69K
Protection level description: Sensor probe body is hermetically sealed and offers complete protection against intrusion of dust, solids and high pressure water or steam into the internal components.
Notes/Considerations: Sensor cable and M12 connector with IP69K rating are needed for complete protection.

Rheonics Devices: DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/), HPHT density and viscosity module
IP Rating: IP69K
Protection level description: Sensor probe body is hermetically sealed and offers complete protection against intrusion of dust, solids and high pressure water or steam into the internal components.
Notes/Considerations: Sensor cable and M12 connector with IP69K rating are needed for complete protection.

Sensor cables with an M12 connector are connected to the probe back end. The IP protection of the cable vary between variants (standard, Ex, High-Temperature, etc.) and will influence the protection rating of the sensor once it is installed. Standard cables have an IP rating of IP68, but IP69K cables are also offered.

To have an IP69K sensor, both the probe and cable should be rated with such protection.

Review all cable variants in this article: Rheonics sensor cable and connection variants (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412899-rheonics-sensor-cable-and-connection-variants).
key_facts: Rheonics SME-DRM sensor electronics are designed for DIN rail mounting in cabinets, offering IP40 protection against solids greater than 1 mm but no water protection.
Rheonics SME-TR and SME-TRD sensor electronics provide IP66 protection against total dust intrusion and temporary flooding for both indoor and outdoor installations.
Rheonics SRV, SRD, DVP, and DVM devices are inline viscometers, density probes, or meters with an IP69K rating, signifying a hermetically sealed probe body.
IP69K protection ensures complete defense against dust, solids, and high-pressure water or steam for the sensor probe body.
Sensor cables utilize M12 connectors and their IP protection rating, which varies by variant (standard, Ex, High-Temperature), directly impacts the overall sensor protection rating.
Standard Rheonics sensor cables have an IP68 rating, but higher-rated IP69K cables are also available.
For a sensor system to achieve an IP69K rating, both the sensor probe and its connecting cable must possess IP69K protection.
warnings_notes: SME-DRM devices are specified for indoor installations or for mounting within cabinets.
To achieve complete IP69K protection for SRV, SRD, DVP, and DVM sensors, an IP69K rated sensor cable and an M12 connector are essential.
The overall IP protection rating of an installed sensor system is influenced by and limited to the lowest IP rating of its individual components, including the cable.

chunks.page_summary: This page lists six references, including an international standard and various Rheonics product pages for inline viscometers and density sensors.
key_facts: Reference [1] points to the IEC 60529 standard, which is related to degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code).
Reference [2] links to Rheonics' inline viscometer product page for the SRV model.
Reference [3] links to Rheonics' inline density meter product page for the SRD model.
Reference [4] links to Rheonics' inline gas LNG density meter product page for the DVP model.
Reference [5] links to Rheonics' HPHT PVT density meter viscometer product page for the DVM model.
Reference [6] links to Rheonics' electronics and communication product page.
warnings_notes: IEC 60529: Degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code)
